sections.heading: Risk Factors

The risk increases with stone size [MS-3].", "strength": "Well-established"}, {"factor": "Porcelain gallbladder", "type": "Primary (for gallbladder cancer)", "detail": "Calcification of the gallbladder wall due to chronic inflammation has been regarded as a risk factor, though subsequent reports suggest the risk (7%–15% cancer) is lower than historical estimates [MS-3].", "strength": "Established"}, {"factor": "Gallbladder polyps (>1 cm)", "type": "Primary (for gallbladder cancer)", "detail": "Larger polyps are associated with a higher risk of malignancy. cards.detail: The presence of chronic inflammation from gallstones is the most prevalent risk factor for gallbladder cancer. The risk increases with stone size [MS-3].

Calcification of the gallbladder wall due to chronic inflammation has been regarded as a risk factor, though subsequent reports suggest the risk (7%–15% cancer) is lower than historical estimates [MS-3].
Larger polyps are associated with a higher risk of malignancy. The Society of Radiologists in Ultrasound recommends surgical consultation for polyps ≥1.5 cm or ≥0.7 cm if indeterminate [MS-3].
A congenital anomaly that allows reflux of pancreatic juice into the biliary tree, causing chronic inflammation [MS-3].
A chronic cholestatic liver disease characterized by inflammation and fibrosis of the bile ducts, significantly increasing the risk of CCA [MS-9].
An association exists, which may be confounded by PSC, as PSC is common in IBD patients [MS-9].
Chronic gallbladder inflammation due to Salmonella typhi carrier state is a risk factor [MS-3].

cards.factor: Hepatolithiasis (chronic calculi of the bile duct)
cards.detail: Stones in the intrahepatic bile ducts cause chronic inflammation and are a well-established risk factor for CCA [MS-9].
Congenital dilations of the bile ducts are a well-established risk factor for CCA [MS-9].

cards.factor: Liver fluke infections (Opisthorchis viverrini, Clonorchis sinensis)
cards.detail: Parasitic infections endemic in Southeast Asia are well-established risk factors for CCA [MS-9].
A risk factor for intrahepatic CCA, similar to its role in hepatocellular carcinoma [MS-9].
A risk factor for intrahepatic CCA [MS-9].

cards.type: Risk factor for iCCA and eCCA
cards.detail: A systematic review showed association with increased incidence of both intrahepatic and extrahepatic CCA [MS-9].
Identified as a risk factor specific to intrahepatic CCA [MS-9].
Identified as a risk factor specific to intrahepatic CCA [MS-9].
Identified as a risk factor specific to intrahepatic CCA [MS-9].
Identified as a risk factor specific to intrahepatic CCA [MS-9].
A potential, albeit controversial, risk factor [MS-3].
